From Discovery to a Monitored Prompt Set

AIO turns a vast, noisy prompt universe into a focused set worth tracking — then hands it to LLM Optimizer to run as a live program.

What this phase does
  • Discovers the right prompt space
  • Narrows the universe
  • Prioritizes the real gaps
  • Curates the monitored set
What it produces

An LLMO-ready monitored prompt set

A focused, deduplicated list of the AI questions that matter most — small enough to monitor well, broad enough to matter.

AIO decides what to monitor; LLM Optimizer turns it into an operational program.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

What This Phase Is Trying to Produce

The output isn't a research dump — it's a small, deliberate set that earns its place in LLM Optimizer.

Not the goal
  • A long, raw research list
  • A keyword dump
  • A broad universe of “interesting” prompts
The goal

A curated monitored prompt set

Relevant to the business, informed by competitors, balanced across recover / expand / defend — and formatted so LLM Optimizer can import it.

A weak input set creates noisy monitoring; a strong one creates clear priorities.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

The Whole Path: One Continuous Loop

Discovery and optimization form a single cycle across Semrush AIO and Adobe LLM Optimizer. This deck zooms into steps 1–4 — turning discovery into a monitored prompt set.

© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

First, the Vocabulary

Three words anchor everything that follows — if you only remember these, the rest makes sense.

Prompt

A question put to an AI

The actual wording someone types into ChatGPT, Gemini, or Perplexity.

best budget family tent
Topic

A cluster of related prompts

Many prompts about one theme, grouped so you can see demand at the theme level.

Family tents
Monitored prompt set

What you hand to LLMO

The curated, deduplicated list of prompts you will track and optimize for — the deliverable of this whole phase.

Keyword research found pages; prompt research finds the questions AI answers.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

The Numbers Behind the Data

Every AIO screen reports the same handful of metrics. Here's what each one means in plain terms.

AI Visibility Score
How often AI answers mention you, scored against competitors0–100
Topic Volume
Estimated monthly AI-search activity for a topice.g. 1,200 / mo
Mentions
Count of prompts where your brand appears in the answer textper brand
Audience
Estimated reach across all topics where you appeartotal reach
Intent
Why the question is asked — informational, commercial, or transactionalcommercial = buying
A high score on a low-volume topic matters less than showing up where demand is.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

One Question, Three Forms

The word “prompt” means something different at each stage — from raw demand, to a measured candidate, to a curated test case you optimize against.

Real customer question
AIO candidate prompt
LLMO monitored prompt
Think of it as
Raw demand, in the wild
A measured pattern
A test case to optimize against
What it is
A spontaneous question, phrased a thousand ways
A cleaned representative of many similar questions
A prompt you commit to track and optimize for
How many
Effectively infinite
Tens of thousands of candidates
Hundreds to a few thousand
Data attached
None — just the words
Volume, intent, brands, sources
Visibility, opportunities, status over time
What you do
Nothing — you can't see it
Research and prioritize it
Monitor, optimize, measure
Treat each monitored prompt as a test case — one pattern that stands for many real questions you want to win.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Step 1 · Understand — Visibility Overview

Your starting dashboard has three parts. Read all three before narrowing anything — this step decides where to point the program.

The gauge

AI Visibility Score

Your 0–100 score versus competitors. Always switch from Worldwide to your home market — the number changes a lot.

The chart

Mentions by Model

Which AI platforms cite your category most. Note the leader — you'll reuse it later to set LLM Optimizer's coverage.

The table

Performing Topics

The topics where your category is active. Scroll it to spot the dominant cluster — that's your focus-area candidate.

Orientation isn't a baseline to track — it's how you choose where to focus.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Step 2 · Explore — The 4-Condition Filter

Set your primary market first (the home-market view is far more accurate), then apply this Advanced Filter consistently across every tab.

Topic contains
Your focus area — a product category, not a brand; it later becomes your LLMO Categorye.g. Camping
Topic Volume
Keeps only topics with meaningful AI-search demand> 500
Brands
Strips crowded, low-signal topics where too many brands compete< 30
Exclude
Drops sub-categories that don't belong to your focus arearemove off-topic
Apply the same four filters on every tab — consistency keeps the views comparable.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Why the Filter Matters

Without it you're staring at tens of thousands of topics. With it, each tab becomes a workable shortlist. (Real counts from a Camping example.)

Before filtering

The whole universe

Every topic AIO has for your market — far too many to read, let alone monitor.

Effectively infinite
After filtering

Topic Opportunities

Camping topics over the volume floor where your brand is absent — your expansion pool.

~ Tens of thousands prompts
After filtering

Performing Prompts

Camping prompts you already appear in — a much smaller set to defend.

~ A few hundreds to thousand prompts
Your focus keyword (here, Camping) is the same value you'll later use as the LLMO Category.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Step 3 · Prioritize — Competitor Research

A separate AIO section where you pit your domain against named rivals to find exactly where they win and you don't.

How to set it up

Add 2–3 rivals

Enter your domain plus a few direct competitors (AIO suggests them), then apply the same four filters.

What it shows

Strong / Weak / Missing

Tabs split the results: Strong (you lead), Weak (you trail), and Missing (rivals appear, you don't). A Mentioned column names which rival.

What to take

The Missing tab

Sorted by Topic Volume, Missing is your highest-value gap list — especially prompts where several rivals appear at once.

Competitor Research turns a candidate list into a strategic one.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Build the Three Tiers

Don't build from one prompt type. A strong set blends three sources — each from a specific AIO tab you've already seen.

Recovery + Expansion + Defense
Tier 1 · Recovery

Competitor gaps

From Competitor Research → Missing. Rivals are cited, you're not — the highest-priority ground to win back.

Tier 2 · Expansion

Open opportunities

From Visibility Overview → Topic Opportunities. Nobody owns these yet — favor commercial intent.

Tier 3 · Defense

Existing strengths

From Visibility Overview → Performing Prompts. You already appear — maintain and deepen it.

Recover lost ground, expand into the open, and defend what you hold.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

The Three Tiers, in Practice

One focus area — Camping. Each row below becomes one line in the final import file.

Tier
Comes from
Example prompt
Why it's in the set
Tier 1 · Recovery
CR → Missing
best budget family tent
Rivals cited, you're not
Tier 1 · Recovery
CR → Missing
most durable 4-season tent
High-volume gap to recover
Tier 2 · Expansion
VO → Topic Opportunities
best cooler for multi-day camping
Open; commercial intent
Tier 2 · Expansion
VO → Topic Opportunities
family camping checklist
Promising theme to expand
Tier 3 · Defense
VO → Performing Prompts
where to buy camping gear online
You appear — hold it
Tier 3 · Defense
VO → Performing Prompts
beginner backpacking gear guide
A strength to deepen

CR = Competitor Research · VO = Visibility Overview

Six prompts, three tiers — this is the shape of a curated set.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Prioritize by Intent

Especially for Tier 2, favor prompts that signal a buying decision over ones that signal idle curiosity.

Favor · commercial / transactional

Buying-stage questions

  • “best [product]”
  • “where to buy [product]”
  • “cheap [product]”
  • “[product] near me”
Deprioritize · informational

Idle-curiosity questions

  • “how does [X] work”
  • “what is [X]”
  • Unless your strategy targets education
Intent lives in the export's topic_intents column, even when it's not obvious on screen.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

What Makes a Prompt Worth Monitoring

A prompt earns one of the limited slots when it combines several of these.

01

Relevance

Clearly tied to your product, category, or use case.

02

Intent

Commercial or transactional — “best,” “where to buy,” “near me.”

03

Competitive value

Reflects a real gap or opportunity vs. rivals.

04

Actionability

Your team could realistically optimize for it.

05

Owned-surface link

Maps to real pages, topics, or initiatives.

06

Program balance

Improves the recover / expand / defend mix.

The test: why does this prompt deserve one of the limited slots?
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Weak Prompts vs. Strong Prompts

A monitored prompt is a test case: realistic, specific, repeatable. Weak ones fail one of those — and the fix is rarely just “longer.”

Problem
Weak prompt
Strong prompt
Why the strong one wins
Too vague
camping gear
best cooler for multi-day camping
Specific enough that answers don't drift
Too unnatural
sleeping bag cold weather best cheap
what's the best sleeping bag for cold-weather camping?
Reads like a real question, not a keyword
Missing context
which one's better?
is a budget or premium family tent better for car camping?
Self-contained — each prompt runs on its own
Too many tasks
best tent, how to pitch it, and what to pack
best budget family tent for car camping
One job per prompt keeps results stable
Low intent
how does a tent work
where to buy a 4-person tent online
Buying-stage prompts are the ones cited
Hard to own
what is camping
beginner backpacking gear checklist
Maps to a real page you can optimize
The goal isn't more prompts — it's prompts worth tracking.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Step 4 · Import — Curate the Final Set

Filters don't catch everything. A manual pass is where the set becomes operationally usable.

Remove · manual relevance pass
  • Non-English prompts that slipped the geo filter
  • Location-specific prompts (rival stores, events)
  • Offerings your business doesn't provide
  • Wrong-context matches — “camping” in a festival topic
Balance & dedupe
  • Dedupe across tiers, keeping the Tier 1 version
  • Cap each topic at a few prompts for breadth
  • Lead with recovery and expansion; defense lighter
  • Sort by topic volume within each tier
Curation is not admin work — it's where the set earns its slots.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

What an AIO Export Contains

When you click export, AIO gives you a CSV with a dozen columns. You'll keep only a few — but it helps to know what they are.

The question

prompt

The exact question someone asked an AI.

The cluster

topic_name

The topic the prompt belongs to.

Demand

topic_volume

Estimated monthly AI-search activity.

Who appears

mentioned_brands

Which brands the AI named in its answer.

Sources

sources_count

How many domains the AI cited.

Intent

topic_intents

Informational, commercial, or transactional.

An export can carry a dozen columns — the import needs only four.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Reformat to Four Columns

Keep two columns, add two, delete the rest. This is the exact shape LLM Optimizer expects.

Keep two, add two
  • Prompt ← AIO prompt column
  • Topic ← AIO topic_name
  • Category ← your focus keyword (you add)
  • Market ← a country code (you add)
Delete the rest
  • topic_volume, mentioned_brands, sources
  • llm, gap_mentions, topic_intents, categories
  • Then save as CSV with a header row
  • An LLM can combine & reformat in one step
Header row, exactly: Category, Topic, Prompt, Market.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Two Kinds of Monitoring

Once the set is live, AIO and LLM Optimizer both report a visibility score — but they measure different things.

Semrush AIO

Strategic market lens

Used at the start and quarterly. Measures visibility against the full 200M+ prompt database — a market-wide signal.

LLM Optimizer

Operational scoreboard

Tracked weekly and monthly. Measures visibility against your curated set only — an operational signal.

Don't compare scores across the two — they measure different universes.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Keep the Set Current

Treat the prompt set as living. Refresh on a fixed cadence and whenever the landscape shifts.

Quarterly cadence
  • Re-run discovery with the same filters
  • Re-run Competitor Research → Missing
  • Re-import the updated set into LLMO
  • Drop prompts you no longer track
Refresh on triggers
  • A competitor launches a product or campaign
  • A major AI platform or model update
  • Your brand launches, enters a market, or rebrands
  • An unexplained drop in AI visibility
The AI search landscape moves fast — a stale set quietly loses relevance.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.

Summary: From Discovery to a Monitored Set

Better discovery and curation up front create cleaner monitoring and clearer priorities downstream.

01 · Understand & narrow

Frame, then filter

Visibility Overview orients you; the 4-condition filter cuts the universe to a focus area.

02 · Prioritize

Build the tiers

Competitor Research (Missing) plus Topic Opportunities and Performing Prompts form the three-tier set.

03 · Curate & reformat

Make it usable

Relevance pass, dedupe, and reformat into Category / Topic / Prompt / Market.

04 · Hand off & refresh

Run the loop

Import to LLM Optimizer, monitor the live set, and refresh it quarterly.

AIO's job: move from a large discovery space to a set LLMO can operate.
© 2026 Adobe. All Rights Reserved. Adobe Confidential.
